Sessions.description: List of all sessions to run. Sessions contain the actions performed against the tested system and its underlying infrastructure in order to receive response data from the tested system to assert on.

Name.description: The name of the session, used to uniquely identify it and its output

Stage.description: The stage of the session. Sessions with the same stage runs together. stage defaultly gets the index of the session in session list

RunUntilStage.description: Optional stage number that decides when the runner waits for this session to complete. If omitted, the session becomes visible only after its own stage completes. If set, the runner defers waiting until the configured future stage is reached.

Category.description: The category of the session, you can filter which categories to run using the -I flag

SaveData.description: Whether or not to save the session's output, if false any data is discarded after its iterated over and the SessionData as a whole is not saved.

TimeoutBeforeSessionMs.description: The time in milliseconds to wait before the session starts

TimeoutAfterSessionMs.description: The time in milliseconds to wait after the session ends

Consumers.description: List of all consumers to build and run for this session. Consumers use protocols to receive data from the application

TimeoutMs.description: The consumption timeout in milliseconds (timeout is the time since last message was read by the consumer)

DataFilter.description: How to filter the properties of each returned consumed data

Body.description: Whether to keep the `Body` in the data (true) or filter it (false)

Timestamp.description: Whether to keep the `Timestamp` in the data (true) or filter it (false)

MetaData.description: Whether to keep the `MetaData` in the data (true) or filter it (false)

Stage.description: The stage in which the Consumer runs at

Policies.description: List of policies to use when communicating with this action's protocol

LoadBalance.description: This policy is in charge of controlling the rate in which the action is repeatedly executed

Rate.description: The amount of actions to perform every `TimeIntervalMs` milliseconds

TimeIntervalMs.description: The time in milliseconds to perform `Rate` actions in

IncreasingLoadBalance.description: This policy is in charge of controlling the rate in which the action is repeatedly executed and increasing it overtime

StartRate.description: The initial amount of actions to perform every TimeIntervalMs milliseconds

MaxRate.description: The maximum amount of actions to perform every TimeIntervalMs milliseconds

RateIncrease.description: How much to increase the rate every RateIncreaseIntervalMs

RateIncreaseIntervalMs.description: How often to increase the rate by RateIncrease in milliseconds

TimeIntervalMs.description: The time in milliseconds to perform `Rate` actions in

AdvancedLoadBalance.description: This policy executes actions in separate stages, each stage has a rate in which to execute the actions included in it and a count or timeout to know after how many actions or after how much time to end the stage and move to the next.

Stages.description: The stages of publishing information, in each stage the messages will be publishedwith a given rate untill 'Amount' messages are generated or untill 'TimeoutMs' is reached

Rate.description: The amount of actions to perform every `TimeIntervalMs` milliseconds

Amount.description: The number of times to perform action

TimeoutMs.description: The time in milliseconds before stopping the communication action

TimeIntervalMs.description: The time in milliseconds to perform `Rate` actions in

VirtualHost.description: Rabbitmq virual host to access during this connection

ContinuationTimeoutSeconds.description: Amount of time protocol operations (e.g. queue.declare) are allowed to take before timing out in seconds

RequestedConnectionTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout setting for connection attempts in seconds

HandshakeContinuationTimeoutSeconds.description: Amount of time protocol handshake operations are allowed to take before timing out in seconds

ExchangeName.description: Name of the exchange to read messages fromCannot be set if configured QueueName to read from.

RoutingKey.description: Routing key of messages to read

QueueName.description: Name of the queue to read messages fromCannot be set if configured ExchangeName to read from.

CreatedQueueTimeToExpireMs.description: The amount of milliseconds before the created queue is deleted when it has no readrs

KafkaTopic.description: Consumes messages from a kafka topic

HostNames.description: List of the kafka hostnames (each hostname should contain the port too for example: - 'host1:8080'

Username.description: Kafka Service with read permissions for the topic's username

Password.description: Kafka Service with read permissions for the topic's password

SaslMechanism.description: The Sasl mechanism used in the kafka

TopicName.description: Name of the topic to read messages from

GroupId.description: The group name to be used when reading messages

AutoOffsetReset.description: Where the reader starts reading from in the topic when being created, latest means it starts reading only messages received after it started reading, other options such as Earliest can be found online.

SessionTimeOutMs.description: The maximum amount of time in milliseconds the reader can go without sending a heartbeat to the reader group coordinator before its considered dead

EnableAutoCommit.description: Whether the read mechanism will commit the offsets automatically and periodically in the background.

PartitionAssignmentStrategy.description: The name of a partition assignment strategy to use. The elected group leader will use a strategy supported by all members of the group to assign partitions to group members. Options: [ `CooperativeSticky` - Ensures that a reader retains its exsting partitions unless it fails, providing stability in partition ownership` / `Range` - Distributes partitions evenly across readers by assigning each reader a range of partitions / `RoundRobin` - Assigns partitions to readers in a circular order, distributing them sequentially ]

MaxPollIntervalMs.description: Maximum allowed time between calls to read messages for high-level readers. If this interval is exceeded the reader is considered failed and the group will rebalance in order to reassign the partitions to another reader group member

FetchMinBytes.description: Minimum number of bytes the broker responds with. If `FetchWaitMaxMs` expires the accumulated data will be sent to the client regardless of this setting.

FetchWaitMaxMs.description: Maximum time the broker may wait to fill the Fetch response with `FetchMinBytes` of messages.

S3StorageClass.description: S3 Storage Class Definitions. Options:[`Glacier` - The GLACIER storage is for object that are stored in Amazon Glacier. This storage class is for objects that are for archival purpose and get operations are rare.  Durability 99.999999999 / `Outposts` - The OUTPOSTS storage class for objects stored in a S3 Outpost / `Standard` - The STANDARD storage class, which is the default storage class for S3.  Durability 99.999999999%; Availability 99.99% over a given year/ `DeepArchive` - S3 Glacier Deep Archive provides secure, durable object storage class for long term data archival. It is the ideal storage class to make an archival, durable copy of data that rarely, if ever, needs to be accessed. It can be used as an offline backup for their most important data assets and to meet long-term retention needs. / `IntelligentTiering - IntelligentTiering makes it easy to lower your overall cost of storage by automatically placing data in the storage class that best matches the access patterns for the storage. With IntelligentTiering, you don’t need to define and manage individual policies for lifecycle data management or write code to transition objects between storage classes. Instead, you can use IntelligentTiering to manage transitions between Standard and S-IA without writing any application code. IntelligentTiering also manages transitions automatically to Glacier for long term archive in addition to S3 storage classes.` / `ReducedRedundancy` - REDUCED_REDUNDANCY provides the same availability as standard, but at a lower durability.  Durability 99.99%; Availability 99.99% over a given year. / `GlacierInstantRetrieval` - Constant GLACIER_IR for ObjectStorageClass / `StandardInfrequentAccess` - The STANDARD_IA storage is for infrequently accessed objects. This storage class is for objects that are long-lived and less frequently accessed, like backups and older data.  Durability 99.999999999%; Availability 99.9% over a given year. / `OneZoneInfrequentAccess` - The ONEZONE_IA storage is for infrequently accessed objects. It is similiar to STANDARD_IA, but only stores object data within one Availablity Zone in a given region.  Durability 99.999999999%; Availability 99% over a given year.]

AssertionConfiguration.title: OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults
AssertionConfiguration.description: Checks for all json items in a configured output that their content is valid according to a csv results file.The results file contains the expected value of fields in each json item and is provided from the configured DataSources.Expects the output items to be deserialized to any type of C# object that can be converted to Json.`DataSources`: Used for loading the csv results file.`Session Support`: Only supports a single session assertion.

OutputName.description: The name of the output to validate its items' content

ResultsMetaDataStorageKey.description: The storage key of the dataSource item of the results. If no key was provided, takes the first item of from the DataSource

DataSourceName.description: The name of the dataSource of the results item. If no name was provided, takes the first DataSource supplied

ColumnNameToFieldPathMap.description: The mapping for each column's name in the outputs results and its matchig field's path in the output json.

JsonConverterType.description: The type of json converter to use for converting the output items

CompareRowsNotInOrder.description: If true, rows can match expected results in any order instead of by their index

anyOf.title: OutputDeserializableTo
anyOf.description: Checks that all items in a configured output can be deserialized with a configured deserializer. `DataSources`: Not used. `Session Support`: Only supports a single session assertion

Assertion.description: The name of the assertion to use

AssertionConfiguration.title: OutputDeserializableTo
AssertionConfiguration.description: Checks that all items in a configured output can be deserialized with a configured deserializer. `DataSources`: Not used. `Session Support`: Only supports a single session assertion

OutputName.description: The names of the output who'se items should be deserializable

Deserialize.description: The deserializer all output items should be deserializable with. Options are all available `QAAS.Base.Serialization` deserializers

Deserializer.description: The deserializer type to use for deserializing. Null means no deserialization will happen. Options are all available `QaaS.Framework.Serialization` deserializers

SpecificType.description: Configuration for making deserializer deserialize into a specific C# object, if set to null will deserialize to default deserilizer's C# object

AssemblyName.description: The name of the assembly the type is located in, If no value is given by default tries to take the entry assembly

TypeFullName.description: The full name (including path) of the type

anyOf.title: ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage
anyOf.description: Checks for metrics' hermetics by comparing the hermetics' percentage based on the `metric hermetics formula` to the hermetics percentage based on the outputs' count to inputs' count percentage `DataSources`: Not used. `Session Support`: Supports multiple sessions assertion. CommunicationData objects with same names will be referenced both by InputNames or OutputNames

Assertion.description: The name of the assertion to use

AssertionConfiguration.title: ValidateHermeticMetricsByInputOutputPercentage
AssertionConfiguration.description: Checks for metrics' hermetics by comparing the hermetics' percentage based on the `metric hermetics formula` to the hermetics percentage based on the outputs' count to inputs' count percentage `DataSources`: Not used. `Session Support`: Supports multiple sessions assertion. CommunicationData objects with same names will be referenced both by InputNames or OutputNames

OutputNames.description: The names of the outputs to sum the counts of

InputNames.description: The names of the inputs the sum of outputs should be a given percentage of the sum of

InputsAreOutputs.description: Whether the given inputs in InputNames are actually another outputs list

MetricOutputSourceName.description: The name of the output to take the metrics from

Tolerance.description: The tolerance of difference between metrics` hermetics percantage and outputs' count to inputs' count hermetics percentage

InputMetricName.description: The name of the input metric of the metric hemetrics formula

OutputMetricName.description: The name of the output metric of the metric hemetrics formula

ProcessMetricName.description: The name of the process metric of the metric hemetrics formula

CombineMetricName.description: The name of the combine metric of the metric hemetrics formula

FilteredMetricName.description: The name of the filtered metric of the metric hemetrics formula

SplitMetricName.description: The name of the split metric of the metric hemetrics formula

anyOf.description: Supports custom hook implementations that are not part of the mirrored common package set.

Assertion.description: The name of the assertion to use

AssertionConfiguration.description: Implementation configuration for the assertion, the configuration given here is loaded into the provided assertion dynamically.

Links.description: The links generated on test results, used to view observability data outputted by the tested application. These links are generated per test result to be relevant specifically to that test and the time it ran at

Name.description: The display name of the link in the test results, if none is given uses the `Type` as the name

Kibana.description: Links the kibana's discovery filtered for the test's session times to each test result.

Url.description: The kibana's url, the base url without any route

DataViewId.description: The Id of the desired data view to view

TimestampField.description: The name of the main timestamp field to use to query on specific times in given data view

KqlQuery.description: A custom Kql query to add to the generated URL, this query is added to the session time filtering query with `and`

Prometheus.description: Links the prometheus' graph filtered for the test's session times to each test result.

Url.description: The prometheus' url, the base url without any route

Expressions.description: The expressions to generate prometheus panels for

Grafana.description: Links the grafana dashboard filtered for the test's session times to each test result.

Url.description: The grafana's url, the base url without any route

DashboardId.description: The Id of the desired dashboard to view

Variables.description: The variables to display the dashboard with

MetaData.description: The metadata for the tests' run

Team.description: The team responsible for the tests

ExtraLabels.description: Extra labels added by the user
